jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's characteristic frustrated exclamation opening the episode
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase itself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific but not exceptional in this context

jokes.text: It's a Krusty Kinda Kristmas! Brought to you by ILG- selling your body's chemicals after you die.
jokes.setup: Christmas show sponsorship announcement
jokes.punchline: Morbid corporate sponsor that harvests body chemicals
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark satire of corporate sponsorship, subversive take on Christmas commercialization

jokes.text: Hey, I thought Krusty was Jewish. Christmas is a time when people of all religions come together to worship Jesus Christ.
jokes.setup: Bart questions Jewish Krusty hosting Christmas show
jokes.punchline: Krusty's contradictory explanation that Christmas unites all religions to worship Jesus
jokes.explanation: Smart religious satire with perfect logical contradiction, highlights commercial Christmas hypocrisy

jokes.text: So tell your folks, 'Buy me Bonestorm or go to hell!' Buy me Bonestorm or go to hell!
jokes.setup: Video game commercial's aggressive sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately repeating the inappropriate demand
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart's impressionability and the commercial's inappropriate influence

jokes.text: Young man, in this house we use a little word called 'please.'
jokes.setup: Bart's demand to go to hell
jokes.punchline: Marge's mild correction focusing only on politeness, missing the profanity
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge character comedy - her selective attention to manners while missing bigger issues

jokes.text: Those are all good points, but the problem is they don't result in me getting the game.
jokes.setup: Marge lists valid reasons against buying the violent expensive game
jokes.punchline: Bart's purely selfish logical framework dismissing all concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - completely self-centered but delivered with deadpan reasonableness that makes it funnier

jokes.text: When I was your age I wanted an electric football game more than anything in the world. And my parents bought it for me, and it was the happiest day of my life. Well, good night.
jokes.setup: Homer relating to Bart's desire, seemingly building to helpful advice
jokes.punchline: Story ends with Homer getting what he wanted, then abruptly leaving Bart hanging
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and classic Homer obliviousness to how his story affects Bart

jokes.text: Mom, I'm not a little kid anymore. Tuck-in time is lame.
jokes.setup: Marge's elaborate bedtime routine with song
jokes.punchline: Bart's resistance to being babied
jokes.explanation: Standard kid-growing-up humor, sets up character dynamic but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, if loving my kids is lame, then I guess I'm just a big lame.
jokes.setup: Bart calling tuck-in time lame
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet but grammatically awkward defense
jokes.explanation: Endearing Marge character moment with mild wordplay on 'lame'

jokes.text: Mom, it's lame to be proud of being lame.
jokes.setup: Marge accepting being 'lame'
jokes.punchline: Bart's meta-criticism of her logic
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation with Bart's kid logic applied to abstract concepts

jokes.text: Well, life is like a box of chocolates. Mom, no! Mom! You never know what you're gonna get.
jokes.setup: Marge starting to quote Forrest Gump
jokes.punchline: Bart's horror at the cliche, but she continues anyway
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about overused movie quotes, Bart's reaction makes it funnier

jokes.text: Allow me to summarize the proposed transaction. You wish to purchase Bonestorm for 99 cents. Net profit to me, negative $59. Oh, oh, please take my $59. I don't want it. It's yours.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to buy $70 game for 99 cents
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's elaborate sarcastic breakdown of the economics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy character voice with mathematical precision applied to sarcasm

jokes.text: Eh, eh, eh! Seeing as we are unfamiliar with sarcasm, I shall close the register at this point and state that 99 cents is the rental price.
jokes.setup: Bart not getting the sarcasm
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's condescending explanation while delivering more bad news
jokes.explanation: More Comic Book Guy superiority, good character consistency

jokes.text: Though I do have a surprising abundance of Lee Carvallo's Putting Challenge.
jokes.setup: No copies of popular game available
jokes.punchline: Abundance of obviously terrible golf game
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the episode's recurring terrible game, 'surprising abundance' is perfect phrasing

jokes.text: This is great! And all I've done is enter my name-Thrillhouse!
jokes.setup: Milhouse playing the coveted video game
jokes.punchline: His pathetically excited reaction to just entering his dorky username
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character moment - his username 'Thrillhouse' became iconic

jokes.text: Uh- Uh, it's only a one-player game. Then how come it says 'second player score'? Mom! Bart's swearing!
jokes.setup: Bart wanting to play, Milhouse lying about single player
jokes.punchline: Caught in lie, immediately tattles about unrelated swearing
jokes.explanation: Good kid behavior - caught lying, immediately deflects with tattling

jokes.text: No, Mom, you idiot! I have Bloodstorm and Bonesquad and Bloodstorm II, stupid!
jokes.setup: Mother asking if he already has Bonestorm
jokes.punchline: Bratty kid's abusive response listing similarly violent games
jokes.explanation: Shows escalated kid entitlement, but one-note spoiled brat humor

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, honey. We'll take a Bonestorm. Get two! I'm not sharing with Caitlin.
jokes.setup: Mother apologizing to abusive child
jokes.punchline: Kid immediately making more demands
jokes.explanation: Continues the spoiled kid theme but doesn't add much new

jokes.text: Four-finger discount, dude. Shoplifting is a victimless crime like punching someone in the dark!
jokes.setup: Explaining shoplifting to Bart
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical comparison to obviously violent crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - 'punching someone in the dark' is such a specific, obviously harmful example that it perfectly shows the flawed reasoning

jokes.text: Go ahead-a, Bart. Take-a the Bonestorm. The store, she's so rich she'll-a never notice.
jokes.setup: Cartoon devil on shoulder encouraging theft
jokes.punchline: Italian stereotype accent making moral corruption sound cheerful
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of devil/angel on shoulders with Italian accent twist

jokes.text: Duh, it's the company's fault for making you want it so much
jokes.setup: Justifying shoplifting
jokes.punchline: Blaming the victim logic that shifts responsibility to marketing
jokes.explanation: Good satire of responsibility-shifting. Captures how people rationalize bad behavior by blaming others

jokes.text: Don't do it, son. How's that game gonna help your putting?
jokes.setup: Angel on shoulder giving moral guidance
jokes.punchline: Focuses only on golf improvement rather than theft being wrong
jokes.explanation: Subverts angel/devil trope by having angel give practical rather than moral advice

jokes.text: Sir, would you open your coat, please? Uhh, I don't think this is the kind of coat that opens.
jokes.setup: Security catching shoplifter
jokes.punchline: Bart's pathetically literal evasion attempt
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - literal interpretation to avoid consequences

jokes.text: Tsk, tsk, tsk, tsk. That boy's parents must've made some terrible mistakes. Shut up, Mom!
jokes.setup: Woman judging Bart's parenting
jokes.punchline: Her own kid immediately demonstrating bad parenting results
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic juxtaposition - judgmental parent immediately contradicted by her own kid's behavior

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Troy McClure. You might remember me from such public service videos as Designated Drivers: The Lifesaving Nerds and Phony Tornado Alarms Reduce Readiness.
jokes.setup: Troy McClure's signature introduction format
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous specific titles of previous PSA videos
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure running gag with particularly absurd video titles

jokes.text: I'm here today to give you the skinny on shoplifting, thereby completing my plea bargain with the good people at Foot Locker of Beverly Hills.
jokes.setup: Troy doing educational video about shoplifting
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's only doing it as part of his own legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of educational format with Troy's sketchy background

jokes.text: Shoplifting began here in ancient Phoenicia. Thieves would literally lift the corner of a shop in order to snatch the sweet, sweet olives within.
jokes.setup: Educational explanation of shoplifting history
jokes.punchline: Completely literal interpretation of 'shoplifting' as lifting actual shops
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal wordplay on 'shoplifting' with absurd historical context

jokes.text: Oh, Sheheqazaramesh, will you ever learn?
jokes.setup: Ancient Phoenician shoplifting scenario
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous made-up ancient name delivered with sitcom frustration
jokes.explanation: Absurd ancient name with modern exasperated tone

jokes.text: If I wanted smoke blown up my ass, I'd be at home with a pack of cigarettes and a short length of hose.
jokes.setup: Brodka rejecting Bart's excuse-making
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely literal interpretation of the 'smoke up your ass' idiom
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor making abstract idiom disturbingly literal, perfect character voice

jokes.text: If you ever set foot in this store again, you'll be spending Christmas in juvenile hall, capisce? Well, do you understand? Everything except 'capeesh.'
jokes.setup: Brodka's threatening ultimatum in Italian
jokes.punchline: Bart understanding everything but honestly admitting confusion about the Italian
jokes.explanation: Good kid honesty undermining the dramatic threat

jokes.text: Lousy, stinking piece of crap. Gotta change that answering machine tape! Oh, God, I gotta change that tape!
jokes.setup: Bart realizing parents will get security message
jokes.punchline: Panic escalating to include changing Maggie
jokes.explanation: Standard panic escalation but the Maggie line is random

jokes.text: Gotta change Maggie! Dear God, we gotta change Maggie!
jokes.setup: Bart panicking about changing the tape
jokes.punchline: Homer mishears and thinks it's about changing baby's diaper
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Homer's obliviousness. Multiple meaning of 'change' creates confusion

jokes.text: Hello, Muddah. Hello, Faddah. Here I am at Camp Granada. Marge, is Lisa at Camp Granada?
jokes.setup: Old comedy song on answering machine
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal confusion about Lisa's whereabouts
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literalness with old comedy reference

jokes.text: 'Tis the season, Marge! We only get 30 sweet, noggy days. Then the government takes it away again.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning why Homer bought eggnog instead of milk
jokes.punchline: Homer's conspiracy theory about government eggnog control
jokes.explanation: Good Homer paranoia mixed with his love of seasonal foods

jokes.text: If you ever set foot in this store again, you'll be spending Christmas in juvenile hall - juvenile hall - juvenile hall
jokes.setup: Bart remembering Don's threat
jokes.punchline: Echo effect emphasizing his worst fear about getting caught
jokes.explanation: Good representation of Bart's anxiety. Echo effect visualizes how threat haunts him

jokes.text: Wow! The March 8 newspaper! Cool! A book of carpet samples! Come on, new bike! Ohh. A soiled wig.
jokes.setup: Kids receiving lost and found Christmas presents
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mundane to disgusting 'gifts'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from disappointing to gross, continues nightmare theme

jokes.text: Come on, new bike! Ohh. A soiled wig
jokes.setup: Kid hoping for something good from lost and found
jokes.punchline: Gets disgusting used wig instead
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure. Contrast between hope and gross reality

jokes.text: What's the matter with your face? Is that a fake nose? Are you wearing chin putty?
jokes.setup: Bart trying to disguise himself for family photo
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific questions reveal his terrible disguise attempt
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of bad disguise with Marge's observant questioning

jokes.text: I hope you're going to the Valley Vista Try-N-Save, kid, 'cause you don't want to come to my store. Catfish?
jokes.setup: Security guard recognizing Bart even in car
jokes.punchline: Abrupt shift to asking about catfish
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist ending undermines the dramatic tension

jokes.text: Uh-oh! Somebody's got tired little legs.
jokes.setup: Bart acting exhausted to avoid going into store
jokes.punchline: Marge's babying response to obvious acting
jokes.explanation: Standard parent-child dynamic, shows Marge's nurturing nature

jokes.text: Now she'll really be surprised when she opens that ironing board cover.
jokes.setup: Marge admiring expensive watch she wants
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely unrelated terrible gift plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to his wife's actual desires

jokes.text: Hello, I'll- Oh, it's just air
jokes.setup: Photographer using squeaky toy to make baby smile
jokes.punchline: Toy deflated, making pathetic sound
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy gag. Functional but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Oh, come on! Hurry up! Okay, people. One, two, three. I thought I told you: no returns for busted merchandise.
jokes.setup: Family photo session
jokes.punchline: Security guard interrupting to treat Bart like damaged goods
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with dehumanizing retail metaphor

jokes.text: I'm afraid your son broke the 11th Commandment: Thou shalt not steal.
jokes.setup: Brodka explaining Bart's crime
jokes.punchline: Calling theft the '11th Commandment' when it's actually one of the original ten
jokes.explanation: Mild religious humor showing character's imperfect knowledge

jokes.text: But someday he'll be a grown man stealing stadiums and- and quarries.
jokes.setup: Predicting Bart's criminal future
jokes.punchline: Escalating to impossibly large theft targets
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation, 'quarries' is particularly funny choice

jokes.text: Haven't you learned anything from that guy who gives those sermons at church? Captain What's-his-name?
jokes.setup: Homer lecturing Bart about morality
jokes.punchline: Can't remember the minister's name, calls him 'Captain'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer religious ignorance, 'Captain' as random title is inspired

jokes.text: Why do you think I took you to all those Police Academy movies? For fun? Well, I didn't hear anybody laughing! Did you? Except at that guy who made sound effects.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming Police Academy movies were educational
jokes.punchline: Admitting nobody laughed except at Michael Winslow
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about bad movies, Homer's reasoning is perfectly flawed

jokes.text: Except at that guy who made sound effects. Honk, honk!
jokes.setup: Homer saying nobody laughed at Police Academy
jokes.punchline: Remembers Michael Winslow character and does impression
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's selective memory and love of lowbrow humor. Somewhat undercuts the previous joke's perfection

jokes.text: Her heart won't just wipe clean like this bathroom countertop. It absorbs everything that touches it like this bathroom rug.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining how Marge processes emotional hurt
jokes.punchline: Using bathroom cleaning supplies as metaphors for emotional absorption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor using mundane bathroom items for complex emotional truth

jokes.text: First, he's grounded. No leaving the house, not even for school. Second, no eggnog. In fact, no nog period. And third, absolutely no stealing for three months.
jokes.setup: Homer announcing Bart's punishment
jokes.punchline: Escalating from reasonable to absurd, treating stealing as temporary restriction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic treating stealing as temporary privilege rather than permanent moral rule

jokes.text: What? I'm more worried about piranhas. Did you see that movie where they sent a nuclear submarine to fight the piranhas and one of them swims right down the periscope and bites the guy in the eye?
jokes.setup: Bart asking about parental love concerns
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's complete non-sequitur about piranha movies
jokes.explanation: Typical Milhouse random tangent, shows his misplaced priorities

jokes.text: Ahh, it got boring. I'm really into this cup and ball now. Man, you never know which way this crazy ball's gonna go!
jokes.setup: Bart claiming video game got boring
jokes.punchline: Fake enthusiasm for the world's simplest toy
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between high-tech game and primitive toy

jokes.text: But can I hang out with you while you do mom stuff?
jokes.setup: Bart desperate for maternal connection
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asking to observe 'mom stuff' from someone else's mother
jokes.explanation: Touching and awkward moment showing Bart's emotional need

jokes.text: Tell me I'm good.
jokes.setup: Bart desperately seeking approval from Mrs. Van Houten
jokes.punchline: Pathetically direct request for validation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical expression of Bart's emotional desperation

jokes.text: Check it out, boy. It's like looking into a living snow mirror.
jokes.setup: Family made snowman versions of themselves
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-profound description of obvious resemblance
jokes.explanation: Homer trying to sound poetic about simple snow family

jokes.text: Look what I swiped from Try-N-Save. It's a replacement tire for a wheelbarrow.
jokes.setup: Jimbo showing off stolen goods
jokes.punchline: Completely useless and mundane theft
jokes.explanation: Anticlimactic theft, shows how pointless the stealing really is

jokes.text: Oh, Bart. I can't believe you did this. I wanted to surprise you for Christmas.
jokes.setup: Marge catching Bart hiding something again
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was hiding a gift for her, not stolen goods
jokes.explanation: Good emotional misdirection, shows Bart's genuine love despite earlier mistakes

jokes.text: Welcome to Lee Carvallo's Putting Challenge. I am Carvallo. Now choose a club. You have chosen a three-wood. May I suggest a putter?
jokes.setup: The terrible golf game from earlier finally being played
jokes.punchline: Overly formal interface that ignores player choices
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to earlier setup, perfectly captures bad game design

jokes.text: You have chosen a three-wood. May I suggest a putter? Three-wood
jokes.setup: Playing the golf video game
jokes.punchline: Game suggests correct club, player ignores advice and chooses wrong one
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to earlier setup. Shows how boring the alternative game is, and player's stubborn wrong choices

jokes.text: Ball is in parking lot. Would you like to play again? You have selected 'no.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the bad golf game sequence
jokes.punchline: Impossible bad result followed by game deciding player's choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the terrible game, 'ball is in parking lot' became iconic
